original: The crude asphalt of this tarmac is one sign of many that these landing pads were thrown together in a hurry. It’s already been cracked and patched in dozens of places, though you have to look carefully under the ever-present neon glow to see it. Flickering holographic displays bid you journey south to an overcrowded taxi stand - the quickest way to venture into the city proper. Many small advisories in black print advise you of danger, but they hardly seem important next to retina-scorching adverts for safe, natural Bright Water.\n\nOther ships park nearby, but the local security is closer to what you’d expect in the core - you can’t access anyone else’s ship without express clearance.
context: <<POS:08083337>> textify(['The crude asphalt of this tarmac is one sign of ma'...])

original:
The taxi stand itself is little more than a concrete rectangle divided by two smallish, six-inch thick windows with cheap speaker grates in the center. It’s plenty busy thanks to the Rush and the appallingly bad customer service being dispensed from the other side. Nearby automated kiosks offer you the choice to flag down a taxi instead of waiting in smoggy, customer service hell with the rest of the taxi depot’s needier clientele.

There’s not much else to look at in the middle of the vast, recently constructed landing pad aside from a rotating cast of inaccessible ships. Not much worth breathing here either, unless you’re looking to add smog to your list of acceptable inhalants.

Your vessel lies north. [flags.DIZZY_BOUGHT|[flags.DIZZY_BOUGHT_TALK||There is a rather worried-looking white-furred kaithrit with dark gray tails and a silver collar huddled in a Paragon-branded overcoat stood near the boarding ramp. You should talk to her before you leave.]]
context: <<POS:08085226>> textify(['The taxi stand itself is little more than a concre'...])

original: Another blocky taxi station just like the one you initially landed at sits here, though this particular facility sits almost square in the middle of the street. Corporate security looms over it from behind like an overly protective parent, though this particular parent seems to have closed off the streets to the north for some reason or another. If you have business in that direction, you’ll need to get clearance from Dhaal’s zaibatsu overlords or a specific destination to taxi to well on the other side...
context: <<POS:08088453>> textify(['Another blocky taxi station just like the one you '...])

original: Suspended roughly twelve feet above a street intersection is a gigantic holographic advertisement for a “Utopian Skies Virtual Vacation.” It consists of buxom alien babes wearing little more than patches on string and a mesh tail-covering - positively scandalous, compared to local norms. When it flickers and loops back to the start, you catch a glimpse of the dingy, pollution-stained city beyond.\n\nStreets lead north, south, and west from here.
context: <<POS:08089265>> textify(['Suspended roughly twelve feet above a street inter'...])

original: Gouts of steam vent out of grates in the pavement with alarming regularity, forcing you to dodge around them or sputter through them. Locals, you notice, don’t even bother to react when catching a face full of concentrated humidity, fully used to the idiosyncrasies of their city’s heat exchange systems. The glossy look it leaves on their skin doesn’t hurt.\n\nThis road bends north and east around a Paragon Inc-affiliated noodle stand. You keep walking when you realize the noodles are moving on their own.
context: <<POS:08090471>> textify(['Gouts of steam vent out of grates in the pavement '...])

original: Sputtering holograms above a cheap, plastiboard noodle stand draw the eyes - and at least a half-dozen natives - to ‘Scented Noods.’ Purportedly it’s a reputable establishment that’s stocked with the finest flavor additives Bright Water has to offer. It actually smells kind of nice... until you get a whiff of orange-and-chemical tang. Such concoctions clearly weren’t designed with Terran sensibilities in mind. Among the graffiti here is an arrow painted in curling lines, pointing south down the street. A cartoonish cow’s face is painted below it. Maybe it would be best to keep walking west or south...
context: <<POS:08091770>> textify(['Sputtering holograms above a cheap, plastiboard no'...])

original: Three stories of curvaceous, holographic zaika loom over top of you, stretching out her arms in invitation. Distant speakers carry the reverb-laden voice to your ears: “Sticky ports? Get your jacks cleaned at ‘Implantation Implant Station,’ now serving Rushers! Searchable on Dhaal-web, and even the Extra-net!” Sure enough, the hologram appears absolutely laden with cybernetics: a poster child for artificial endowments.\n\nThe alleyways run south or north through the advertisement’s legs.
context: <<POS:08093332>> textify(['Three stories of curvaceous, holographic zaika loo'...])

original: A food truck leans off-balance against the curb, pockmarked with rust. The drives are long gone, replaced with cheap metal struts. It won’t be going anywhere, but it doesn’t need to. A near-constant crowd lines up for a taste of authentic Ausar cuisine, but the meat on a spit in the window looks suspiciously like one of the local rodents. Some of the graffiti here looks less like the usual crude drawings and more like an arrow, done all in curling lines. A cartoony udder dripping milk is drawn beneath it. The arrow points north.\n\nThe street winds north and west.
context: <<POS:08094710>> textify(['A food truck leans off-balance against the curb, p'...])

original: Elderly zaika cluster on a balcony a floor up, leering down at the streets over the armor-plated railing. Their shrill voices chatter almost constantly, loudly enough to be heard over the din of the city, disparaging the young and cybernetically enhanced alike. Clouds of steam burst out of nearby vents well enough to obscure you from their ire, though you can’t help but flinch when one erupts out of nowhere. The locals, as they walk down streets to the north, east, and south, barely react.\n\nHow long would you have to live here to be so blasé about it?
context: <<POS:08096142>> textify(['Elderly zaika cluster on a balcony a floor up, lee'...])

original: An ancient-looking hospital surplus hoverbed wobbles in the brightest part of the street. Its owner stands behind it, waving twenty mechanized digits from her cybernetic hands and offering her services as a “ChipDoctor”. Projections ring her in a halo of services and prices: 99 credit recaps, 79 credit overclocks, 500 credit owner-tag rewrites, and a host of other quasi-legal services that would only interest a poor cyborg who can’t replace her parts. Back alley doctors really do come in all flavors.
context: <<POS:08098341>> textify(['An ancient-looking hospital surplus hoverbed wobbl'...])

original: This road once housed a bakery, some decades back. The signage is still mostly legible. You can’t make out the alien glyphs for its full name, but “CLOSED FOR RENOVATIONS” still reads as clear as day, even in perpetual twilight. On Terra, a place like this would be boarded up. The zaika were a bit more thorough. Steel plates gird the dessicated storefront against gang shenanigans. You suppose you can’t smash a window you can’t even see, though the locals sure have tagged the hell out of it with graffiti. Is that one a picture of one of a tailpussy as a metal flower? You suppose “Razor Lotuses” would make for a badass gang name... The street stretches north and south while an alley rests to the west.
context: <<POS:08099198>> textify(['This road once housed a bakery, some decades back.'...])

original: You find yourself in a leisure district of sorts, with an assortment of small bars, snack stalls, vice cliques, cathouses, gambling clubs and other places whose purpose you can’t and probably don’t want to know. Probably. At any rate, everything here is small-scale, substandard and decadent, but you can’t deny there is a certain dystopian allure to it.
context: <<POS:08100944>> textify(['You find yourself in a leisure district of sorts, '...])

original: A propaganda poster featuring a zaika riding a tank full of milk has been hung up here. You could swipe it. Nobody would care.
context: <<POS:08101466>> output("A propaganda poster featuring a zaika riding a tank full of milk has been hung up here. You could swipe it. Nobody would care.")

original: Thumping bass lines issue from the building to the west - four of them, each pounding out its own alien tempo in direct contradiction of its peers. It’s exactly the kind of discordant racket you’d need earplugs to endure - or a case of criminal insanity. Flickering strobes of light emerge from half closed windows in ever-shifting hues, but you don’t have any intention of joining in, not after seeing the street level doorwomen. Gang names and symbols flash from industrial holoprojectors mounted on robotic shoulders.\n\nDoes it smell a bit like... milk here? No... it doesn’t matter. This is a gang house, and you have no business here. The street streaks north and south away from here.
context: <<POS:08102294>> textify(['Thumping bass lines issue from the building to the'...])

original: The street dips down to snake under an overpass’s worth of gigantic pipes. Condensation drips steadily from the largest, stamped “BrighterWater.” You’re pretty certain you could drive a hovertruck down the center of it without touching the sides, and a hovercycle would fit perfectly in the smallest one. Another bears “U.S. Datapipe” - an odd label. Secondary text warns that vandalism or damage is punishable by “involuntary limb-reassignment.” The net-pipe plunges directly into the closest concrete wall, vanishing behind a pair of high voltage transformers.\n\nDhaal’s asphalt artery flows on to the north and south.
context: <<POS:08103992>> textify(['The street dips down to snake under an overpass’s '...])

original: This street’s holo-sign is almost completely invisible beneath a brighter, orange-fuzzed projection from Paragon Inc’s security division: “In light of elevated crime in this area, this sign has been equipped with redundant security systems and multiple recessed overwatch turrets. Further attempts at theft will be met with lethal force. Have a wonderful day courtesy of your friends at Paragon Inc!” Your Codex’s translation reveals that one of the words in the street’s name is frequently used as a euphemism for the extremely perverse act of penetrating one’s own eggslit with her tail. That would explain the theft. Teenagers never change... though lethal sniper turrets seem to have put a stop to their chicanery for now. The street bends east and south.
context: <<POS:08105552>> textify(['This street’s holo-sign is almost completely invis'...])

original: Zaika and Rushers mingle together, owing to the taxi station and numerous alien-friendly establishments in the region (if all the advertisements are anything to go by). Stylized cow-girl holograms beg for you to sample their wares a little further north. Other amenities, like a capsule hotel, can likewise be found that way. You can almost ignore the pan-handlers spilling their stories to other aliens with their palms outstretched for a taste of the core’s fabled wealth.\n\nThe street splits in a three way T-intersection to the north, east, and south.
context: <<POS:08107940>> textify(['Zaika and Rushers mingle together, owing to the ta'...])

original: A propaganda poster featuring an attractive zaika in a pair of VR-glasses is hanging here. You could swipe it. Nobody would care.
context: <<POS:08109186>> output("A propaganda poster featuring an attractive zaika in a pair of VR-glasses is hanging here. You could swipe it. Nobody would care.")

original: With a twilight sky and stratospheric buildings on every side, you can see why Dhaal is considered every bit as much an urban jungle as Terra herself. Lit by a looping advertisement for the new line of “Salivation” branded nutrient broths, this shadowy road has enough dark alleys and nooks to hide two or three gangs worth of miscreants. Best get to your destination in a hurry. The Gyre is not a safe place.\n\nA brightly lit taxi stand offers a haven for Rushers like yourself just to the east. Maze-like streets burrow through the monolithic skyscrapers like cunt snakes tunneling beneath customs checkpoints.
context: <<POS:08109680>> textify(['With a twilight sky and stratospheric buildings on'...])

original: A cube-like chunk of armor with reinforced trans-aluminum windows squats in the shadows between skyscrapers. Blindingly bright advertisements flash through rainbows of transportation-themed advertisements meant to draw the eye while a ten-barreled, starship-class turret broadcasts an entirely different message: don’t fuck with Paragon Inc’s taxis.\n\nThe streets open up a bit to the west.
context: <<POS:08111192>> textify(['A cube-like chunk of armor with reinforced trans-a'...])

The air reeks worse than a garbage dump. To the ").concat(e,", the wall of piled detritus prevents any form of land-bound travel. The owners of several franchised garbage trucks appear to be in an argument with a pair of mechanics in Bright Water jumpsuits about who has jurisdiction over the mess - and the payout.")

original: The comforting ambience of Arovok, the famed milk bar surrounds you. Incredibly busty cowgirls strut from table to table offering milk from their glasses or their barely-concealed tits, administering a half-drugged crowd of colorful natives and spacers alike. Presently, you sit near the bar, considering your next move.
context: <<POS:08113563>> textify(['The comforting ambience of Arovok, the famed milk '...])

original: Hissing steam pipes leave this part of the city wrapped in a thick fog. Ghostly shapes of neon advertisements flicker all along the narrow path, while the lights of swoop bikes and flying taxis trace spectral contrails above. More than a few zaika have tucked themselves into the nooks of the oppressive buildings, faces buried in handfuls of alien drugs. Despite the stifling steam and hazy fog, you can make out the flash of fresh chrome on a couple of denizens who pass you by.\n\n\t\tIt’s hard to see much, but the street seems to go north or south.
context: <<POS:08114463>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tHissing steam pipes leave this part of the c'...])

original: Glistening sheets of polished copper are suspended on roughly welded, patchwork metal pillars in a sort of make-shift gate. A neon sign projected across its length reads: “The Short Stacks,” though it’s hardly needed. The square is filled with gabilani and raskvel of all sorts and up to all manner of things: squabbling, hauling carts of scavenged scraps, haggling, and hawking souvenirs. An impromptu open-air market has been thrown together by the gabilani, carts and booths of all shapes and sizes blockade the street. Quite a few dusky-skinned natives peruse the smaller aliens’ wares, looking oddly out-of-place on their own homeworld. The ever-social gobs are all too eager to wave you in as well. “Hey Rusher! Wanna be a cowgirl for the milk bars? I’ve got the best stuff! You could be making bank in a matter of minutes!” “Acid rain wearing you down? Buy one of these acid-proof ponchos! One size fits all!” “Cocksocks! Cocksocks for sale! Made for a zaika, but fashionable enough for a rusher!” And on and on. All a bunch of typical tourist junk, most likely. The space is crowded, but the streets lead in all four directions.
context: <<POS:08115949>> textify(['Glistening sheets of polished copper are suspended'...])

original: This section of the street has been overwhelmed by little food carts, advertising their specialties in bright, garish colors. Throbbing neon lanterns have been strung overhead, from one building to the next, lighting up the corridor with a festive appearance. The customers here are as likely to be natives or rushers as the gabilani themselves.\n\nThe busiest booth seems to be a family-run business titled “Bun in the Oven.” Similar-looking gobs scurry from customer to customer, taking orders or delivering large, plump, milk-bread spheres stuffed with various fillings. At the center of the scramble stands a plentifully pregnant gabilani matriarch, cooking each order in a custom-built, portable bakery. Considering the size of her barely apron-covered breasts, you can guess where she gets the milk from.
context: <<POS:08117327>> output("This section of the street has been overwhelmed by little food carts, advertising their specialties in bright, garish colors. Throbbing neon lanterns have been strung overhead, from one building to the next, lighting up the corridor with a festive appearance. The customers here are as likely...

She offers you a big, sympathetic grin before one of her offspring drops a delivery, turning her attention back to the business at hand. She chucks a plastic spoon at him with pin-point precision and begins re-making the customer’s order. Never a calm moment for the materfamilias!

She flicks the big, jingly jewelry hanging from her ears and flutters her dusky eyelids in your direction. If she weren’t already fit to burst, you don’t doubt the amorous matron would be eager to sample what you’ve got to offer!

original: The road goes east, deeper into the Short Stacks’s Red Light district, or west, to the noisy bazaar.
context: <<POS:08118926>> output("\n\nThe road goes east, deeper into the Short Stacks’s Red Light district, or west, to the noisy bazaar.")

original: The sounds of the western crowds are only a dull roar in this part of the gabilani-settled slum. The streets grow more narrow and the neon lights are a dimmer, reddish hue. Despite the comparatively less mobbed conditions, it feels like the ambient temperature has gone up a few significant degrees. There’s a perfumed scent all through the low, narrow, buildings clustered on either side of you. There’s no shortage of gobs in this part of town, but they’re all hanging out inside, standing in front of tall, pink-lit windows. Judging by their suggestive clothing, hungry glances, and lewd postures, they’re not just admiring the planet’s perpetual dusk. You can see a couple of zaika in one of the domiciles to your right wave the girl in the window their way, the lights turning out when she steps away. To your left, a discreetly hooded peer steps into the window-room, wraps her tail around an effete gabilani boi’s neck like a leash, and pulls him away from his post.\n\n\t\tThose not choosing to sample the pleasures of the Red Light district can head south or west.
context: <<POS:08119347>> textify(['The sounds of the western crowds are only a dull r'...])

original: Though this section of the neighborhood lacks the beaming pink neon of the Red Light district to the north, the implications remain in the lewdness of the holographic projections adorning their store signs. There’s a massage parlor that advertises their hot oil rubdown as ‘orgasmic,’ a micro-bar that promises ‘an intimate experience,’ and an arcade whose sign just shows a filled condom being tossed through a hoop that looks like the female gender symbol. Hawkers outside try to get your attention and draw you into their client’s business, eager to milk as much Rusher money out of you as they can.\n\n\t\t\tNorth leads to the Red Light district, while south takes you toward the less salacious parts of the Stacks.
context: <<POS:08120886>> textify(['Though this section of the neighborhood lacks the '...])

original: You’re deep in the commercial section of the Stacks. Though the lighting is more intimate than abrasive, every storefront has more than its fair share of glowing signs. The locals mill about, chatting on their way about town. You can feel their eyes on you, however, as the gabilani warily eye outsiders. Judging by the occasional smashed window and suspiciously crude condemned notice, you’d guess they’ve got good cause to be distrustful of outsiders. It seems like most of the shops here are unofficially ‘locals only,’ but there are at least one or two that still have an inviting vibe.\n\nThe road goes north and south from here.
context: <<POS:08122370>> textify(['You’re deep in the commercial section of the Stack'...])

original: Much of the flash and showy decor of the more forward-facing parts of the Stacks have given way to unassuming buildings. This seems to be a more residential part of the slums, with simple buildings rising up, up, and up in rough-and-ready apartments. Though they’re still overshadowed by the corporate megaliths built by the Zaibatsu, the local housing projects are at least impressive in just how quickly they seem to have been erected. Gabilani work ethic, you suppose. \n\nThe ground floor of the apartment buildings seem to have been set up as shops, mostly for the locals, but open to the public all the same. One, a clothing store, even has a hand-made sign outside that invites customers to “Gwon Inside.” The street runs north and south.
context: <<POS:08123398>> textify(['Much of the flash and showy decor of the more forw'...])

original:
This part of the neighborhood is in shambles and seems abandoned. The buildings look scorched by a recent fire, the lights are smashed, and the walls have been crudely painted with nasty messages in a luminescent yellow paint. ‘Get Off Dhaal,’ ‘Fuck Neon,’ and other, far less mentionable complaints and curses have been scrawled just about everywhere. This seems to be the work of some zaika who didn’t care for the Tarkus natives settling on their planet. [pc.nmh
		|You can’t help but shake your head. There’s always going to be someone afraid of the future and takes it out on the least defended segment of the populace. The story’s the same, no matter where you go.
		|You cluck your tongue at the sadly unoriginal graffiti. If you’re going to make a statement, you ought to have something to say. And their brushwork? Abysmal!
		|You scoff at the cowards who did this. Buncha xenophobes who aren’t ready for the galaxy at their doorstep. Pathetic.]

The rubble and detritus make it nearly impossible to get a vehicle through here, but on foot you could go north or west.
context: <<POS:08124360>> textify(['\\n\\t\\tThis part of the neighborhood is in shambles'...])

original: Workshops line this boulevard, buzzing and sizzling with non-stop construction. The ever-inventive denizens of Tarkus have lost none of their stamina in the move to Dhaal. The air is full of the sound of gobs hard at work behind forges, running machine shops, or operating elaborate and exotic tools in the endless quest for the riddle of steel. Low-flying hovercraft swoop down and land in the street to offload a mishmash of metals, electronic parts, and fuel. Your attentive gaze catches the driver discreetly pocketing the payoff from the locals, presumably to keep redirecting Zaibatsu shipments off their original courses. \n\nYou keep your eyes skyward, just in case any more vehicles descend through the smog-clogged sky. The road goes west toward bright lights and east toward sullen darkness.
context: <<POS:08125661>> textify(['Workshops line this boulevard, buzzing and sizzlin'...])

original: You’re standing outside ‘If That Don’t Work, Use More Gun’ arms store. There are no windows and the door is a heavy, blast-proof slab of metal. Standing outside the store is enough to make the two heavy machine gun turrets track your movement. You better keep your hands off of your weapons unless you want to get riddled with holes.
context: <<POS:08126698>> textify(['You’re standing outside ‘If That Don’t Work, Use M'...])

original: Gabilani are intensely social and nowhere is this more obvious than their densely packed residential district. Buildings of all kinds are pressed so tightly together that there isn’t room to slide a credit chit between them. Some structures are proper buildings, while others seem to be cargo crates stacked one atop another as high as relative stability allows. There’s plenty of scavenged stone and steel making up ramshackle dwellings in one part of the neighborhood, while others seem to be hollowed-out spacecraft. Even the street itself is winding and uncertain, zigging and zagging up and down the way to create extra room for more apartments and homes.\n\nBeaming, corporate lights stream from the south, while the north is more pleasantly subdued.
context: <<POS:08127248>> textify(['Gabilani are intensely social and nowhere is this '...])

original: What were once zaika-built structures have now come into the possession of a number of well-to-do gabilani. These homes have glowing hardlight windows, reinforced doors, security cameras, and well-tended, nocturnal gardens hanging down from their roofs. The street is clean and lit with low-intensity bulbs. A fair number of zaika walk up and down the street, admiring the homes of the affluent or paying a visit to a friend of the Zaibatsu, but the gabilani and raskvel who pass through keep their heads down and their steps brisk. It’s clear who’s welcome in this part of the Stacks and who isn’t. Seems like it pays to know people in high places, even when you’re living in the lowest part of the city.\n\n\t\tThe street goes north and south from here.
context: <<POS:08128206>> textify(['What were once zaika-built structures have now com'...])

original: The street market to the north continues down here, though this part of the city runs underneath a massive, overhanging freeway that cuts out even the slightest spec of the roiling, cloudy sky. Because there’s no chance of acid rain, the locals have filled the space with more permanent structures than the rest of the Stacks: holo-mag stands, outdoor bars, elaborate sculptures, and even an oil-spewing fountain. All of it is illuminated with a series of giant, glimmering, blue globes whose metal frameworks slowly rotate around like gyroscopic stabilizers. Which, considering the builders, they may very well be!\n\n\t\tRushers peruse the offerings, the lights above giving everyone a turquoise hue, as if they too were gabilani. The street goes north to the bazaar, east to a construction site, and south to the residential part of the neighborhood.
context: <<POS:08129321>> textify(['The street market to the north continues down here'...])

original: A number of official-looking zaika mill about in the abandoned ruins of a construction site. Their tails whip back and forth in agitation as they survey the scene, loudly complaining about the short off-worlders who seem to have made off with all the equipment and materials intended for the city’s latest construction project. One suggests reporting the loss to the XOs, and letting the Zaibatsu enforcers roust the little aliens out of their boltholes. Another protests that they’ll probably be held accountable for the loss if they speak up. No conclusion is reached, but all agree: the city would be better off without the vulgar little garbage-pickers. \n\n\t\tSteamy alleys lead north, while the main street leads east.
context: <<POS:08130417>> textify(['A number of official-looking zaika mill about in t'...])

original: A poster advertising Paragon’s latest and greatest cybernetic is barely adhered to one of the walls. You could swipe it. Nobody would care.

original: The interior of the store is downright tiny, only accommodating half a dozen people, separated by a counter behind thick, bulletproof glass. All the hardware is behind the counter. There’s a plaque on the glass, telling all customers to keep their weapons holstered, and considering that the interior is also guarded by automatic machine gun turrets, you should do as it says. [flags.MALAI_MET|Malai is sitting behind the counter flicking through a magazine, waiting for customers.|There’s a blue-scaled Raskvel sitting behind the counter, flipping through a magazine as he waits for customers.]
context: <<POS:08131963>> textify(['The interior of the store is downright tiny, only '...])

original: It looks like this used to be a steam station, responsible for regulating and guiding the flow of heat through this part of the city. Judging by the damp, hot clouds of fog spilling out everywhere, you’d guess the station’s not quite as operational as it ought to be. Even a cursory inspection shows that a good many of the surface-level devices have been partially disassembled. Perhaps cannibalized by the scavenging locals?\n\n\t\tThe main street leads east, but you spy an alley way through the mist leading to the south.
context: <<POS:08133015>> textify(['It looks like this used to be a steam station, res'...])

original: Stepping inside the seemingly unassuming clothing store feels like stepping through a portal to journey to a dimension composed entirely of garish color and bombastic fashion. Every wall is decked in clothing of every shape and size. The unifying theme of the collection appears to be ‘loud,’ though if you had to pick a second attribute, it would be ‘sexy’ - not entirely unexpected in the land of exposed breasts and perpetual twilight. The doorway to the east provides an exit.
context: <<POS:08133736>> textify(['Stepping inside the seemingly unassuming clothing '...])

original: The crowds aren’t as dense here, but there’s still plenty of unhappy-looking zaika loitering about. Your cab already left. All you can do is push forward into the throng of bodies and hope for the best.
context: <<POS:08136848>> textify(['The crowds aren’t as dense here, but there’s still'...])

original: The crowd is thicker here, the zaika angrier. You can hear the source of commotion ahead. Someone with a bullhorn is screaming something ahead, if you can just push through to the front. If it weren’t for Feruze and the crackling ball of pain and electricity dangling from her tail, you might’ve found yourself in a spot of trouble. Sometimes it pays to have a bodyguard.\n\t\t\n\t\tYou need to push north.
context: <<POS:08137304>> textify(['The crowd is thicker here, the zaika angrier. You '...])

The sergeant’s leaning against the gate, enjoying her cigar. “Captain,” she says, nodding her head. “Go on in. I’m sure the overseer would like to thank you for the help.” |The rioters have, well, gone to riot with the security forces cleared out. They’re streaming past the gates and slamming into the front doors, pouring inside as fast as the roundabout will let them — you imagine the only reason they aren’t climbing through the windows is because they’re bulletproofed. There’s no sign of the guards you defeated: they’ve been dragged off by the crowd to whatever fate may await them. There’s nothing stopping your coming or going now. ]

context: <<POS:08139749>> textify(['The street stretches right up to the compounds con'...])

original: Whew, you’ve been walking for what feels like forever, but your Codex’s chrono says it’s only been five minutes. The road snakes west and north from here, pinned between the monolithic complex structure and the reinforced concrete walls that guard it. Even shaded, the pavement feels almost blisteringly hot. Distortions in the distance make it hard to see the details of where you’re going.
context: <<POS:08140288>> textify(['Whew, you’ve been walking for what feels like fore'...])

original: Roads... where you’re going... you need roads... and air conditioning. You would kill for some air conditioning right now! No wonder they use heavy trucks to move material back here. It’s like you’re baking in an oven! Worse still, this complex is so big that it takes you forever to get anywhere!\n\t\t\n\t\tThe pavement continues to the north and south.
context: <<POS:08140963>> textify(['Roads... where you’re going... you need roads... a'...])

original: You catch a break from the sun here, shaded by the wall. Dry breezes blast through the artificial canyon, only slightly better than baking in stagnant air. No entrances to the [targetFactionName]’s complex reveals themselves to you yet. Maybe going back to the front would be best?\n\t\t\n\t\tOr you could stubbornly push on... What’s another five minutes in this heat?
context: <<POS:08141634>> textify(['You catch a break from the sun here, shaded by the'...])

original: Now at the northeast corner of the compound, the cargo road takes an abrupt turn. You can see it reaching for an industrial loading dock to the west. To the south, it plows straight ahead through the gap between building and wall, made a virtual oven by inhospitable temperatures. Oh, how you long for the steady twilight of the Gyre!

There aren’t any guards or workers left back here, at least -- you reckon they must all have gone inside when the job was done.

original: [targetFactionName] keeps a tidy compound. Recently waxxed floors shine dully beneath the fluorescent lights above, marred only by the panicked scuffs of recent unrest. Illuminated display cabinets proudly show off a collection of unintelligable awards like “Best Use of Synergistic Co-Dependence in a Corporate Setting” or “Most Luminous Advertisement.” An entranceway lies to the west. This corridor carries on to the east, under the sleepy eye of an unpowered security camera.
context: <<POS:08146834>> textify(['[targetFactionName] keeps a tidy compound. Recentl'...])

original: The broad corridor you’ve been following bends to the north and east from here. A potted plant sits in the corner, glowing faintly. Closer inspection reveals it to be made of plastic, lighted by embedded fiber optics in an attempt to artificially parody bioluminescence. From time to time, it puffs floral-scented mist into the air.
context: <<POS:08147669>> textify(['The broad corridor you’ve been following bends to '...])

original: Locked doors frame your path to the east and west. Offices for minor beauracrats or janitors, you dismiss them as unworthy of the effort t would take to crack them open, notable only as places a hostile security officer or misguided rioter could hide. Neon streamers wave briskly under a hard-working ceiling vent. It can’t be easy keeping this place so cool.

You could breeze on in for a closer look or trundle along the corridor’s length to the north and south.

The walls remain largely unmolested, covered over by inspirational displays for sociopaths.

The walls remain largely unmolested, covered over by inspirational displays for sociopaths.

It might not be a bad spot to take a load off, compared to elsewhere in this dangerous installation.\n\nA crumpled-up flier for a company dining event lies on the floor. It seems proud that the guest fee is only 500 credits.
context: <<POS:08151971>> textify(['Rounding a south-west bend, you expose a whole new'...])

original: Live stock prices flicker on the wall. [targetFactionName] stays locked at the top, of course, updating with sparkly effects when it twitches upward and subtle dissolves when it slides back down. A watercooler bubbles below it, though the little paper cups you expect to find are long gone and the water has a concerning pinkish tint to it. “Ten cup limit!” reads a warning, just above another sign. “You are being watched!”\n\nThis place is starting to remind you of Dad’s MaxCorp stories...
context: <<POS:08153238>> textify(['Live stock prices flicker on the wall. [targetFact'...])

original: “Elevator Access: L2 Employees and Above”\n\nDespite the sign, there isn’t any security set up to stop you from barging in and punching any button you want. This seems like a good route to dig deeper into [targetFactionName]’s corporate guts. Alternatively, signs on the southern wall indicate a break room isn’t far to the east, and executive offices can be found to the west.
context: <<POS:08154594>> textify(['“Elevator Access: L2 Employees and Above”\\n\\nDespi'...])

original: The same sign you saw outside appears again inside: “Blah blah, level 2 employees. Who cares,” but it doesn’t stop you from searching through the options on the keypad. Aside from the usual suspects like ‘close door’ and ‘emergency stop,’ there is only one button worthy of your attention: B1. This complex has a sublevel, and you’re a single press away from gracefully entering its depths.
context: <<POS:08155727>> textify(['The same sign you saw outside appears again inside'...])

original: A dried-on coffee stain lingers on the floor. No doubt it would have been cleaned up long ago, had it not been for the riot, but it’s been left here to mark the site of an accident - or particularly unpleasant news being delivered. Bean-impregnated breezes still tickle at your nostrils when you breathe.\n\nYou can follow the compound’s main corridor east, toward and elevator, or west to the executive offices. The air grows cooler in that direction.
context: <<POS:08156429>> textify(['A dried-on coffee stain lingers on the floor. No d'...])

original: A holo-cart sticks halfway out of the western wall. Muted beeps still emanate from somewhere behind the plastery substance. Shards of a shattered display screen ring the floor around it. Somebody’s going to be losing out on a few paychecks once corporate reviews the security cams...\n\t\t\n\t\tWhy, the executive offices appear to have an entrance just south of here. Maybe they’re worth checking out? Alternatively, you could poke around the elevator that’s a bit further down the hall to the east. Surely it leads deeper into the complex.
context: <<POS:08157694>> textify(['A holo-cart sticks halfway out of the western wall'...])

original: Ornate double doors with bulletproof glass panels guard the entrance to the executive offices, according to the sign just above. Luckily for you, they were left unlatched and gently parted by the riot’s chaos. Nothing would stop you from popping in and searching for some clues.\n\n\t\tOf course there are plenty of other locales to pillage in this complex. Signage seems to indicate an elevator isn’t far around that corner to the north.
context: <<POS:08158614>> textify(['Ornate double doors with bulletproof glass panels '...])

original: An organized desk is a sign of an organized mind. This one is too wrecked to indicate anything other than the power of chaos against long-maintained order. Behind it lies another pair of doors and a staircase to the rest of the executive offices, lavishly indicated by a thick purple carpet of neon intensity. You hope it isn’t radioactive. You can slip back out the security doors to the east at any time... or investigate the offices beyond the stairs to the west.
context: <<POS:08159409>> textify(['An organized desk is a sign of an organized mind. '...])

original: This wide hall stretches through the concrete-reinforced walls of the [targetFactionName] base, north toward the executive offices. To the south lies a corner and the way back to the entrance. Interlocking geometric patterns on the walls flash delightfully in response to your passing.\n\t\t\n\t\tThe suits must have a pleasant walk on their way to work in the morning.
context: <<POS:08160373>> textify(['This wide hall stretches through the concrete-rein'...])

original:
A ladder rests against the corridor wall beneath a burned-out lighting panel in the ceiling, left by a maintenance tech once the chaos of the riots began. The other lights reflect brightly off the freshly waxed floor.
		
		It’s a shame the corporate zaika will never know the joy of sliding around on glossy floors in nothing but a pair of socks.
		
		Maybe when you’re in charge of Steele Tech you can teach them a thing or two.
context: <<POS:08161091>> textify(['A ladder rests against the corridor wall beneath a'...])

original: Though the world may be alien, the instruments of business remain familiar. Here, a timeclock sits on the wall, displaying the local time in alien letters. There’s no slot or dock for physically interacting, so it likely operates via some form of wireless technology to painless track its employees comings and goings.\n\t\t\n\t\tThe air to the east grows warmer, closer to the entrance. The west, it cools to comfort on your way to the executive suites.
context: <<POS:08161882>> textify(['Though the world may be alien, the instruments of '...])

original: The deeper the elevator goes below ground, the more you notice a hum of heavy machinery growing in the background. You feel it rumbling through the floors. It sounds like you’re entering some kind of factory or production factory. No doubt [targetFactionName] produces some of their goods here, where their methods are concealed from the prying eyes of the city. The elevator door opens south into the underground factory complex.\n\nYou can ride the elevator back up to the surface.
context: <<POS:08162694>> textify(['The deeper the elevator goes below ground, the mor'...])

original:
If this security checkpoint was staffed, you might be in trouble. Instead, the scanners sit abandoned. No guard will stop you from slipping between two of the scanners instead of through the indicated aperture. You’re free to scamper deeper into the facility - or peek into the security office to the west.
		
		An elevator back to the surface is to the north.
		
		The factory floor rumbles to the south.
context: <<POS:08164005>> textify(['If this security checkpoint was staffed, you might'...])

original: The security station is as you would expect: desks full of empty caffeinated beverage containers pointed at walls stacked with security monitors. The dozens of feeds provide a more than ample view of the facility, even into the Research and Development wing where you have to make do with blurry black and white footage.\n\t\t\n\t\tYou can exit the office and return to the security checkpoint to the east.
context: <<POS:08164792>> textify(['The security station is as you would expect: desks'...])

original: The factory floor opens up here, though there are enough truck-sized storage containers to make navigating it seem like a maze. Lucky for you, your Codex’s built in topography scanner already mapped it out enough to get around. You’ll just have to keep your eyes out for any patrols in the area.\n\t\t\n\t\tYou can head west, toward a large, crane-shaped machine, or east toward another intersection.
context: <<POS:08165805>> textify(['The factory floor opens up here, though there are '...])

original: A conveyor belt emerges from the north wall. Deprived of power, it sits idle. Half-constructed plastic shells lie upon it, not yet labeled with the flashy product name they’ll come to bear in time. To your west, you can see a cargo crane, big enough to pick up one of the giant crates (like the one blocking the way south) and tote it around the factory.\n\t\t\n\t\tThe factory floor stretches east.
context: <<POS:08166575>> textify(['A conveyor belt emerges from the north wall. Depri'...])

original: The factory floor stretches away to the south and west, around the squared-off corner of a ten foot tall cargo container. Its ceilings rise higher than the cargo tunnel to the north’s, high enough for squat cranes to rearrange the stacked resources as production warrants. A lingering puddle nearly wets your [pc.foot], fed by a steady drip from a pipe above. Despite the label ‘water,’ what emerges is brackish and foul. It smells like it could strip paint.
context: <<POS:08168492>> textify(['The factory floor stretches away to the south and '...])

original: During full operation, this tunnel would be used to stage finished goods from the factory for trucks at the loading bay to the north. Now it lies empty, a back way into the factory itself, which grows louder with each southward step you take. A neon-illuminated sign on the wall reports, “Only 16 accidents today! Great job!”\n\n\t\tThe source of those accidents is clear: rumbling machinery to the south, blasting out its discordant cacophony. A rack of safety equipment hangs on the western wall beneath a sign that reminds you that the company must be compensated for lost labor in the event of an injury. Most of the hardhats are already cracked, whatever they were cast from already flaking apart. Most of the gloves have holes in the fingers. No wonder the workers get injured so often... and riot.
context: <<POS:08169319>> textify(['During full operation, this tunnel would be used t'...])

original: Parked loaders line the western wall of this corridor. You can follow the scuffed rubber tire tracks south or step back outside to the north.
context: <<POS:08170491>> textify(['Parked loaders line the western wall of this corri'...])

original: You stand under the arm of a massive, automated crane. A nearby mass conveyor rolls nearby, toward a sealed port in the wall marked with towering neon words: “RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT DIVISION.” Stacked crates and the factory’s east wall conspire to limit your options to moving north or south from here.
context: <<POS:08171464>> textify(['You stand under the arm of a massive, automated cr'...])

original: A thick wall hems you in to the south. The concrete floor below, cracked and scuffed in a half-dozen places, seems like it was cured too fast to achieve proper hardness. Nonetheless, it bears the weight of the factory’s weighty cargo containers. A wide gap leads west across the battered floor. The heavy arm of a cargo crane rests across the way north, like a crude mechanical archway.
context: <<POS:08172312>> textify(['A thick wall hems you in to the south. The concret'...])

original:
You catch a glimpse of the south wall as you move. It’s heavy stuff, blast-hardened concrete, and in far better shape than the floor. It’s the sort of material you’d expect to see in a military base - and not spent on a factory wall, even one skirting the burning hellfire of Dhaal’s sunward side.

East leads back the way you came.

West continues through shipping containers and conveyors, deeper into the facility.
context: <<POS:08173066>> textify(['You catch a glimpse of the south wall as you move.'...])

original: An auto-packer straddles between two of the thicker crates. The conveyors that should be feeding it sit bare and idle, abandoned in the chaos. Spindles for spools of shrinkwrap stand similarly bare. A tangle of wires spills out of the control cabin like intestines from a gut wound. It won’t be running any time soon, and you can skirt through the gaps to continue west.
context: <<POS:08174285>> textify(['An auto-packer straddles between two of the thicke'...])

original: Just to the south lies an airlock of enormous size. It’s maw could casually devour one of the cargo crates you’ve bee threading through all this time. There’s even a conveyor that feeds directly into it. Above, a heavy loader arm dangles from the tracks. This area is less deserted. Every now and then someone emerges from the airlock. You’ll have to be careful to avoid being seen.

The inside of the warehouse is thankfully devoid of the kaithrit guards you saw outside. This small space seems to have been an office lobby or perhaps the front end of some business before Utopian Skies snatched it up for their private storage. Thankfully, your fate - one way or the other - lies deeper in the warehouse.

A considerable filing cabinet close at hand looks like it’s been cleaned out, probably right before this site got converted into a waypoint for gun runners. There’s a large desk, strewn with memos, invoices, employee reviews, and similar paperwork. Though you just give them a cursory scan, it is plain that things were going poorly for the little people caught up in Zaibatsu schemes. What’s more, the smashed neon tubes in the corner tell you exactly what the former occupant thought of her employers’ desire to make the planet brighter for the sake of the visiting aliens. Small wonder they’re using freelancers instead of keeping the old staff on duty.

Scavenged neon floodlights illuminate their little home-away-from-home, while hammocks strung from one box to the next give the felines a rather casual living space. Small cartons of street food are piled up on a shallow crate in the middle of the space, but most of the kaithrit seem occupied with a huge, glowing screen. It’s hard to tell exactly what they’re watching, but considering the specialties of their employer, you don’t doubt it’s some sort of native smut.\n\n\t\tAs far as the guards themselves, you’ve got eyes on the four kaithrit. Though the four boys are outfitted differently, their casual demeanor and friendly attitudes suggest that they’re part of an established team, not just a mish-mash of hirelings. Dealing with the whole group isn’t going to be as easy as just showing up with a winning smile or a big gun. You don’t have a view of their boss, the kui-tan woman, but from the scene outside, you suspect she’s even less likely to roll over without some vigorous convincing.

Entering the unsealed room, you find yourself in what feels like a tomb. The very air itself tastes like death on your tongue, stale and dust-laden, scratching its way down the back of your throat. A blanket of ashen grime is painted thick onto every surface, stray particles drifting as you make your way inside.

The room is small, and feels confining rather than cozy. The far wall is covered with a mountain of monitors, most of them cracked or otherwise destroyed.
